title: Smoky French Padron Peppers Stir-fry
description: This kid-friendly dish features tender padron peppers marinated in a smoky garlic and herb sauce before stir-frying with onions and shallots for an easy-to-cook whole30-friendly main course.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup chicken broth
ingredients.amount: undiluted, if not using bone-in pieces

instructions: Whisk together olive oil, minced garlic, and smoked paprika in a large mixing bowl.
Add halved padron peppers, tossing to coat with the marinade. Let sit for at least 20 minutes or up to an hour, covered.
In a large skillet over medium heat, sauté shallots and remaining olive oil until translucent and golden brown (about 5 minutes).
Add halved padron peppers directly from the marinade into the skillet. Cook until slightly softened but still crunchy.
Stir in thyme, lemon juice, and salt and pepper to taste. Mix well and cook for another minute or two until heated through.
Divide among plates and garnish with chopped green onions if desired.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
